title: Greater Control about Advanced Filters (and + or)
excerpt:
Hi monday-team, 
I would be really happy about two changes for filters: 


give us the option to combine “and” and “or” filters. So far when changing this dropdown it changes for everything. 
However I would like to be a…

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi monday-team,

I would be really happy about two changes for filters:

  1. give us the option to combine “and” and “or” filters. So far when changing this dropdown it changes for everything.
    However I would like to be able to do for example the following:
    Filter everything assigned to person A with status X OR assigned to person B with status Y
    This should not include person B with status X and vice versa.

  2. include these advanced filters in dashboards. Even without the change in combining “and” and “or” this would greatly improve the use of dashboards, as I understand the current filtering is always an “or-condition”

Thank you for your replies!

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 ON THISSS Onee , its a must-have

posts.author: donaldcornel (Donald Cornel)
posts.cookedHtml: 100% agree to this! Hope to consider this asap.

posts.author: Cvon (Cara Vonlukawiecki)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

Alternatively, or in connection with, would it be possible to provide “dashboard/filter” level access to guests? Instead of subscribing them to entire boards, they would only see the filtered information displayed on the dashboard?

posts.author: TaylorTedesco (Taylor Tedesco)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any updates on this from anyone?

I NEED to be able to use Advanced Filters in Dashboards to be able to get the type of data I need. I am looking to be able to create a chart based off a certain period of time. Currently there is no way that I can find for me to be able to do that.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @TaylorTedesco - Good news! Advanced filters for dashboards is currently undergoing internal testing at monday. No ETA yet on when it will start rolling out to the general user community, but it is coming.

posts.author: TaylorTedesco (Taylor Tedesco)
posts.cookedHtml: Awesome!! Thank you! You’ve been giving me great news lately lol.

posts.author: Tomas (Tomas Iglesias)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey there! Any update here?

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bump on this.
I have more and more instances in which I would need to combine AND and OR filters. Will there be an update to this functionality anytime soon? Or anytime at all, for that matter.

thanks!

posts.author: Danl (Dan'l)
posts.cookedHtml: Completely agree. I joined community just to suggest this. The best way to implement this would be parentheses – a left paren and a right paren could each be one line in the filter. You can limit nesting to keep things from getting OTT complicated, but it really is a must-have; I need to be able to see, for example, features that are assigned to me OR my partner, that have at least one not-done status.

posts.author: BenH (Benjamin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Oh my goodness, I just noticed this. This is still happening that we can’t mix “AND” with “OR” filters on a board. This is such a disruptive restriction.
Are there any updates on the development of this feature?

posts.author: broskisworld (Josh Bosley)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bump! Would love to be able to use this. My use case for reference:

We have a high-level board of projects across all departments and skills. We’re a very flat structure and freelancers and employees often help in different departments.

We often use tags, views, and filters to narrow information and view an approximate to-do list. Example views: “My Tasks” shows Me (Dynamic) AND is not in groups “Done”, “Waiting on Client”. The “Dev Tasks” view currently shows Me (Dynamic), and all of the Devs, OR any tasks in the “Web Design & Development” group. However, I’d like it to do that AND also not in the group “Done”, “Waiting on Client” - that way you can view just current projects.

posts.author: ddelcastillo (Daniel del Castillo)
posts.cookedHtml: I wouldn’t call this a feature but a bug and I am very surprised that this was requested more than a year ago and still is not fixed

posts.author: BenGregory (Ben Gregory)
posts.cookedHtml: Came here trying to work why combinations of AND / OR was working. Only to have it confirmed that this was never implimented in the first place which I think is incredibly surprising and disapointing. I’ve been using Monday for a month or so and so far I’m not impressed with how limiting it is. I’d be using Notion if I had the choice.

posts.author: Kalyani (Kalyani)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am already dealing with adding filters for each widget for 40 boards on a dashboard and now not having ability to use bitwise operators together just adds a layer of disappointment.

Can this please be prioritized?

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have been on a sabbatical for 3 month. It is very disappointing that this topic not only hasn’t been solved but there isn’t even a response from someone from monday.com

Please let us know when the advanced filtering has actually advanced at least this far.

posts.author: ulfhednar (Camille Freking)
posts.cookedHtml:
Still no update here Monday or Polished Geek?

We just transitioned to Monday from Notion and the fact that this isn’t possible is significantly hindering my ability to assemble meaningful data.

I literally have a whole dataset that’s being filtered out of a bandwidth prediction. Yikes.

posts.author: Kristian (Kristian)
posts.cookedHtml:
Also waiting for this.

Until it’s available, here’s a couple of workarounds

Option 1. Use a formula column for advanced conditions and set the filter based on this column
Option 2. Add a tabel widget to a dashboard. AND & OR can be combined by putting separate filters for the dashboard and the widget. (Only a viable solution for less complex boards, but useful in some cases)

image1566×189 14.3 KB

posts.author: Dieter (Dieter Verlaeckt)
posts.cookedHtml: Combining AND and OR would be really useful. If you are worried about managing parenthesis, you could keep it simple first and just give priority to the AND’s over the OR. That covers at least half the use cases already.

posts.author: Kalyani (Kalyani)
posts.cookedHtml: It would also be great if they give us an option to write the query. I think most of us are willing to manage that.

posts.author: EMorton (Erik Morton)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding another steady +1 to the pile for this, since this is definitely comes across as a bug, given that the Support Page for Filters calls out “Note: When adding multiple conditions, you can choose from “AND” or “OR” to allow you to narrow down your results to exactly what you need!” in a helpful little blurb… despite the fact that this bug actually prevents us from actively narrowing down results without using some sort of work around like a formula column.

posts.author: bhorlings (Brian Horlings)
posts.cookedHtml: I couldn’t agree more. This is a must for the advanced features.

posts.author: abonsey (Andrew)
posts.cookedHtml:
Gets my vote too.
Monday.com appear to be dragging their feet on this one

posts.author: Mischmaster (Michael Luber)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 We need that feature too.

posts.author: adhofdfds (Matias Hoffmeyer)
posts.cookedHtml: Absolutely, yes. Cmon Monday, you can do it! And-or-filters for the win, should be doable and quite important since it affects all boards?

posts.author: gwsavuk (Graham Wright)
posts.cookedHtml: +1! Or at least change the name, as these are the least “advanced” filters I’ve ever encountered.

posts.author: clessard (Cameron Lessard)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there an update on this? Thanks!

posts.author: Momo (Monique de Goeij)
posts.cookedHtml: yes we really need this! @monday-team do you have an update?

posts.author: skyline (Project Manager)
posts.cookedHtml:
You should be able to make filters by both “AND” & “OR”.
It shouldn’t make all of the conditions be either “AND” or “OR” if you only choose one.
This is weak filtering. Conditions should be able to be “AND” and “OR” where applicable.

image692×383 26.5 KB

posts.author: phouse (Peter)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree. Not having the option for both is an unnecessary limitation.

posts.author: MaricaM (Marica Mody)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello, I would like to use both And & Or when filtering, but it only allows me to use 1 filter type.
For Dashboard, I would like to view Owners are XYZ OR Subtask Owners are XYZ AND Status is XYZ.

How am I able to use both when filtering?

posts.author: GeoffAirey (Geoff Airey)
posts.cookedHtml:
I want to show tasks with selected statuses on two different status columns (i.e. an OR of two different field values) but also want to And that whole piece with those due to start after a certain start date.
e.g.
(((Priority = “Priority”) or (Status = “Working on it”)) AND (Earliest Start Date Is on or before TODAY ))

This would be easy if the Filters allowed nested conditions.

posts.author: too_hawt_to_handle (Eli Kent)
posts.cookedHtml: How does monday.com not have this? How are there only 9 votes for this? This is such a basic need.

posts.author: aarondisca (Aaron Merryman)
posts.cookedHtml: You can actually do this if you use the rest API manually. It looks they just don’t expose it with the query builder. Be warned, updating it this way will cause the query builder to be broken until it’s reverted to a supported structure.

posts.author: hughmacken (Hugh M.)
posts.cookedHtml:
Please add filter operators like “contains,” “does not contain,” “is empty,” or “is not empty” for text fields and filter operators like “is greater than” or “is less than” for number fields.

Also, please add boolean AND and OR operators to allow for more refined searches when filtering across multiple fields.

As an example, I have a lookup column. And I have a status column. And I want to be able to easily create a saved view for all the rows where the lookup column field is empty OR the status column value is Done.

Thanks!

posts.author: PedroM (Pedro Malaga - monday.com Certified Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @hughmacken,

In the meantime, if you’re not doing that yet, you may use a new formula column with an IF/OR to filter those values and then select this column as a filter in your board filter.

posts.author: hughmacken (Hugh M.)
posts.cookedHtml: Great idea, thanks Pedro!

posts.author: MHKPhD (Mathilde Hvidtfelt Kjeldsen)
posts.cookedHtml: I came here to request exactly this flexibility in the filter function! It is really lacking!

posts.author: safddsafsfsfsfsadfsf (KP)
posts.cookedHtml: Thought I’d throw my hat in here and (or) create an account just to say it’s been 2 years. Any progress? I think this has got to be a bug.

posts.author: amiraenergyedc (Amira R)
posts.cookedHtml: +1!! I need this on the dashboard to help with reporting! please and thank you

posts.author: lindduke (Lindsey Dukesherer)
posts.cookedHtml: this is majorly needed!

posts.author: maalfunction (Beatriz)
posts.cookedHtml: Monday please hear us on the and + or filter!!

posts.author: Matheus (Matheus)
posts.cookedHtml: Created a community account just to vote on this one

posts.author: oliel.noy (Oliel Noy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Same here. Really need this feature for company dailies as well as faster quick filter for sub task owners.
I also created a community account just in order to reply to this thread.

posts.author: daniel.jarolim (Daniel)
posts.cookedHtml: I came across it during work with Monday and automatically googled community forum issue. This is huge issue for us. So +1 vote.

posts.author: Tommy (Tomas Salamon)
posts.cookedHtml: Can you please add this to Monday as soon as possible, please. We cannot move without it. Thank you.

posts.author: bernhard (Bernhard)
posts.cookedHtml:
I wanted to find out how to do this, thinking I must be missing something obvious, which is how I found this thread.

It’s pretty shocking to learn that monday chooses not to implement such a basic feature. This is “user friendly simplification” to the point of uselessness.

posts.author: James.Deguara (James Deguara)
posts.cookedHtml: Monday, please implement this as soon as possible, it will make the filters sooo much more usable.

posts.author: jfarm (John)
posts.cookedHtml:
still collecting dust after 1000+ days without attention from the Monday.com team

Our culture of transparency & trust, customer centricity, ownership & impact, inclusivity, speed & execution and product-first approach shines through in every aspect of the day-to-day

uh huh…

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone! Thank you for all the valuable feedback. Our dashboards development team, is currently working on adding adding more control to advanced filters on dashboards and are hoping to release this in the coming months and share it with our community.

To be the first to now about the updates make sure to turn your notifications on for this post. How to turn on notifications for a topics

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi John! Last year we launched a new feature request process in which we give the top requested features a status update. You can read more about the new process here: All you need to know about our new Feature Request process

We recently updated many requests and entered many request from the community to our 2024 roadmap! Keep voting and checking back on your favorite features. If you want to be notified of new updates certain requests, make sure to turn on your notifications.

posts.author: amber (Amber from monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey everyone, Amber here from the monday.com team!

As we continue with product discussions in this area, I want to make sure that your voices remain heard at every point in the process and that your insights continue to shape our focus and direction.

I’d love to hear more from you about your ideal vision of how more advanced filtering could best work for you.

What possibilities does the combination of AND +OR filtering open up for your workflows? Are there other functionalities within the filtering experience that you believe could help you work more effectively? Consider things like available fields, the conditional statements (contains / is / is not etc) and the dynamism of the filtering experience.

If you could share more about how you ultimately envision using these filters to achieve more, maybe that could shed some light on what options would work best for different use cases!

Looking really forward to hearing all your thoughts

posts.author: amber (Amber from monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml: @dk_NFroehlich @hlopezvc @TaylorTedesco @PolishedGeek @Danl @BenH @broskisworld @BenGregory @ulfhednar @Kristian would love to hear your thoughts here!

posts.author: amber (Amber from monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml: @GeoffAirey @MaricaM @skyline @gwsavuk @adhofdfds @EMorton @Dieter @hughmacken @daniel.jarolim @Tommy would love to hear your thoughts!

posts.author: vjabbinante13 (Vito Abbinante)
posts.cookedHtml:
In case this wasn’t mentioned: this request should apply not only to Filters, but to Automations as well.

Currently, I have a team of 7 people. The ideal workflow for us would include more than one option to trigger a task…not simply forcing one very specific parameter (the AND function), but asking the system to allow one OR the other to be true in order to run the auto.

Example Automations:
When Status1 changes to ABC…
and only if Status2 is 123 OR 456…
assign Person1 AND Person2 as This Person…
and start TimeTracking1

When Status1 changes to ABC…
and only if Status2 is 987 OR 654…
and only if Dropdown3 is QQQ OR RRR OR SSS…
assign Person3 AND Person4 as This Person…
and change Status3 to <=>

When Status1 changes to ABC…
and only if Status2 is 987 OR 654…
and only if Status3 is &&& OR $$$…
and only if Dropdown3 is QQQ OR RRR OR SSS…
assign Person5 AND Person6 as This Person

When Status9 changes to XYZ…
and only if Person1 OR Person2 are assigned as This Person…
move item to Group1…
and notify Someone1

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
@amber I think my use cases in the opening statement are what I need primarily and @vjabbinante13 pretty much added the only thing that I would need on top.

So simply put a combination of different states/entries of columns should be usable as a trigger for filters and automations.

posts.author: nitzan (nitzan mayer wolf)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi community! Nitzan here from the Monday.com team.

We’re in the process of developing a new feature to address the AND+OR capability discussed in this thread. But we can’t do it without you.
We would love you guys to join a usability session and help us shape the future of filters in Monday.com. Please feel free to reach out for joining to this session
Your feedback matters!

@dk_NFroehlich @hlopezvc @TaylorTedesco @PolishedGeek @Danl @BenH @broskisworld @BenGregory @ulfhednar @Kristian

posts.author: cfriedenberger (Cassandera Friedenberger)
posts.cookedHtml: This request says that it is released, however I can not individually select the and/or option for the advanced filters. It still sets the and/or for all of the filters that are added. Am I missing something?

posts.author: cfriedenberger (Cassandera Friedenberger)
posts.cookedHtml: @monday I am still looking for a response to this question.

posts.author: khayden (Kim)
posts.cookedHtml: Agree with this need.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
How does this have the status “Released”? That is simply not true. If I switch the dropdown on one line of filters, it switches for all lines.
Not even the WorkFlows would cover this feature because there isn’t the option to combine conditions.

posts.author: crushtest (Kostya)
posts.cookedHtml: same here. wanted to use two OR filters, two basically different filters in one - and it’s not working, when I’m adding new filters with AND, and then adding a group to use OR there - I can’t change the rule. It can be only one.

posts.author: Hiraeth (Sue Cornwell)
posts.cookedHtml:
And so we’re now here 4 years later in 2025 and STILL no acknowledgement from Monday?

We also need to be able to compare different project lines. If project name x = not done, and project name y is done etc etc. But we can’t because the basic filtering means one name blocks out the other.

The filtering is so basic it’s borderline useless and while we have 30+ people on the pro membership, we’re now looking at ClickUp because Monday is not giving us what we want at all.

Many of us here would easily be able to write our own queries if there was the framework to do it. Such a shame for an otherwise decent product.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: While I would much prefer if they finally gave a simple combination of and/or in the normal filters, you might want to look at the Workflows feature. This is basically a decision tree branching to "yes"s and "no"s. Which can be the equivalent to “and”. Your "or"s can be integrated by selecting multiple options in a single “node” of the tree.

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone, it is actually true that this feature has been released!

You can go to “Advanced filters” and instead of creating a filter and then adding “New filter” at the bottom you have to choose a “New group” at the bottom. The naming is irritating since the term “group” is used for something else in monday and it would be much easier to understand if it was “filter group” but at least it works.

When choosing a group you will get a box in which you can combine filter conditions. If you now add another “group” you can create a second set of filters. These two sets can have a different choice for “and” and “or” on the top left. You can even combine groups of filters with single filters.

Note that if you already have filters set up, you will have to delete and recreate them inside a group because they will not be in a group.

posts.author: popjaco (Poppy)
posts.cookedHtml: We definitely need this! We want to be able to have a fixed view called “To review” for all projects where either the project status is pending review, or another status is pending review, but then our teams often like to filter this view by region. If they do, it stops the other filters from working. Help!!

posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @dk_NFroehlich
You can achieve this by following filter

posts.author: dk_NFroehlich (Nils Fröhlich)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes I know, I already described that two comments above Greater Control about Advanced Filters (and + or) - #66 by dk_NFroehlich

posts.author: MickaylaM (Mickayla Murray)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone

I’m excited to make it official: Advanced filter groups (with mixed “AND” + “OR” logic) have been released! You can now use “New group” within Advanced Filters to create separate sets of conditions ,each with its own AND/OR logic. You can even combine groups with individual filters. Translation: much more precise filtering, way less “why is this item showing up here?”.

We heard you on the naming, too. “Group” does carry a lot of weight in Monday, and clarity always matters. Feedback like that absolutely gets shared internally. One important callout (as mentioned above): If you already have filters set up, you’ll need to recreate them inside a group to take advantage of the new logic; they won’t automatically convert.

This is one of those power features that quietly levels up dashboards and board views in a big way. Thanks for raising it, testing it, and helping each other along the way. Keep the thoughtful requests coming!
